claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child or children.

**First, let me ask about the age of the child you're claiming for:**

How old is your child? Specifically:
- Are they **under 16 years old**?
- Are they **16-19 years old**?
- Or are they **20 or over**?

(If you have multiple children, we'll go through each one. Let's start with the first child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 15 years old, so yes, he's under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 15 years old (under 16), let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now the next question about Alex:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

In other words, is Alex either not working at all, or working part-time for less than 24 hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's still in school, so he's not in any paid work.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since Alex is not in paid work, let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Next question about Alex:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, is Alex receiving Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name? 

(Most 15-year-olds in school wouldn't be receiving these benefits, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular teenager living at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question about Alex:

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

This includes:
- Any type of apprenticeship in England (Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, or Degree Apprenticeships)
- Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland
- Apprenticeships in Wales (except Foundation Apprenticeships, which are fine)

Since you mentioned Alex is in school, I'm assuming he's not in an apprenticeship, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. Like I said, he's just in school - he's only 15, so he hasn't started anything like that yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Alex is not in an apprenticeship, let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common situation)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, job-seeking, etc.)
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester, so no issues there.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Since you normally live in the UK, let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex:

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You meet this requirement if ANY of the following apply:

1. **You live with Alex** (the most common situation)
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep (paying at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance for Alex under a court order or agreement

Since you mentioned Alex lives at home with you, I'm assuming you live together - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- A former partner

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. If someone else is claiming, they would need to stop their claim before you could start yours.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to understand Alex's living situation:

**Is Alex currently:**
- In hospital?
- In care (looked after by the local authority)?
- Being fostered?
- Or living normally at home with you?

From what you've told me, it sounds like Alex is living normally at home with you. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct. Alex is living normally at home with me. He's not in hospital, care, or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for Alex's housing or living costs? (This would be unusual for a child living at home with a parent, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I'm supporting him myself - he just lives at home with me like any normal teenager.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me get the final outcome for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE**

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for your son Alex.

### Why You Qualify:

✅ Alex is 15 years old (under 16 - automatically qualifies)  
✅ Alex is not in paid work  
✅ Alex doesn't receive benefits in his own right  
✅ Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship  
✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)  
✅ You live with Alex and are responsible for him  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  
✅ Alex lives normally at home with you  
✅ The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation  

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge** - if you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through your tax return
5. **Payments are made every 4 weeks** (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### How to Apply:

You can find the application form and more details here: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is 15 years old, which is under 16 and automatically qualifies; (2) He is not working (still in school); (3) He does not receive any benefits in his own right; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for him; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 15-year-old son Alex who lives at home with them in Manchester. All child qualification criteria and claimant responsibility requirements have been satisfied.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is 15 years old, which is under 16 and automatically qualifies; (2) He is not working (still in school); (3) He does not receive any benefits in his own right; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship; (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester); (6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for him; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 15-year-old son Alex who lives at home with them in Manchester. All child qualification criteria and claimant responsibility requirements have been satisfied.
